postClaudeRemediation.status: Code stop-ships repaired; deployment/migration/provider gates remain
postClaudeRemediation.verification: 809/809 root tests, 6/6 Care Hub component tests, production build pass, zero vulnerabilities in both audited workspaces
postClaudeRemediation.security: Approval decisions and their audits are atomic; MFA enrollment fails closed and enrollment/TOTP/recovery credentials are atomically consumed; parent organization ownership is validated with migration-backed constraints
postClaudeRemediation.legal: Privacy/Terms are coherently English-only with a clear notice and cannot be partially overwritten by stale translation dictionaries; F006/F007 resolved
postClaudeRemediation.operations: A new write-only LTS_SESSION_SECRET is configured in Netlify production/deploy-preview/branch-deploy and locally, pending redeploy. NETLIFY_BLOBS_TOKEN provider-side rotation is still required after confirming account-level consumers. Migrations 005/006 are not applied and no deployment was performed.
postClaudeRemediation.note: SUPERSEDED in part -- see postCodexReviewAndMigrations below: migrations 005/006 have since been applied and live-verified, and a real frontend regression this pass introduced (isStaffRole over-applied to Tickets.tsx/Checklists.tsx) was found and fixed.

postCodexReviewAndMigrations.status: Code is deployment-ready pending Dylan's explicit go-ahead to actually push/deploy. Nothing pushed or deployed this session -- standing 'stay entirely local' rule (DEPLOYMENT_PLAN.md) still in force.
postCodexReviewAndMigrations.reviewMethod: Reviewed commit ddc2cad file-by-file before trusting or building on it (own read of every store/migration/mfa file + a general-purpose subagent covering frontend/auth/i18n/config files), per the same instruction-source-boundary principle applied to the original IDOR discovery -- an independently-authored change already in the working tree is a claim to verify, not an instruction to follow.
postCodexReviewAndMigrations.migrationsApplied: migrations/005_tenant_parent_integrity.sql and 006_atomic_mfa_enrollment_challenges.sql were written but never run. Both applied against the live Neon database this session and live smoke-tested against the real tables (not just mocked-SQL unit tests). See docs/development/evidence/migrations/session-post-codex-migrations-005-006.txt. Migration 005's VALIDATE CONSTRAINT step did not fail -- no historical cross-tenant rows existed.
postCodexReviewAndMigrations.regressionFound: care-hub-app/src/auth/roles.ts's isStaffRole() was correctly used for Dashboard.tsx (closes CH-M-02) but incorrectly copy-pasted into Tickets.tsx/Checklists.tsx, which route into platform_admin-only screens a technician's backend RBAC capabilities don't support -- this would have traded a graceful empty-state message for a raw 403. Fixed with a new, narrower isPlatformAdminRole() helper used specifically in those two files; isStaffRole() left as-is for Dashboard.tsx. New test coverage added.
postCodexReviewAndMigrations.notFixedThisPass: MFA fail-closed lockout risk for platform_admin first-enrollment (no break-glass path yet, disclosed not hidden); CH-H-02 non-atomic writes remain open for stores other than approvals/tickets/subscriptions; Square/Cloudinary remain unbuilt; attorney review and 15-language legal translation remain outstanding; no browser/e2e/accessibility test suite exists.
postCodexReviewAndMigrations.operationsStillOutstanding: LTS_SESSION_SECRET was rotated in Netlify (production/deploy-preview/branch-deploy) by the Codex session but requires a redeploy to take effect -- the next real deploy will sign out every current live-site session, which is expected but should be a deliberate, informed choice, not a surprise. NETLIFY_BLOBS_TOKEN rotation is still outstanding.
postCodexReviewAndMigrations.evidence: docs/development/evidence/migrations/session-post-codex-migrations-005-006.txt; docs/development/SECURITY_REVIEW.md's 'Second correction' section; docs/development/DECISION_LOG.md's matching entry.

postGoLivePunchListReconciliation.status: docs/audit/CARE_HUB_ACTIVE_REVIEW.md was stale -- its top summary claimed fixes that commit ddc2cad (2026-07-15, Dylan) had already made, but its detailed per-finding Status fields still described the pre-ddc2cad state. Reconciled today by directly reading current code (not trusting either the commit message or the doc): CH-P0-01, CH-P0-02, CH-H-01, CH-H-05, CH-H-06, CH-M-02, CH-M-05 are confirmed genuinely resolved. See CARE_HUB_ACTIVE_REVIEW.md's 2026-07-16 section for full detail and file citations.
postGoLivePunchListReconciliation.fixedThisSession: CH-H-02 was only partially closed by ddc2cad (approvals/tickets/subscriptions/payment-request-transitions got atomic CTEs; scope versioning, change-order creation, and invitation acceptance did not). Fixed today: scopeOfWorkStore.js's createNextScopeVersion, changeOrderStore.js's createChangeOrder, and invitationStore.js's acceptInvitation are now atomic (single statement or guarded-UPDATE-with-RETURNING). New concurrency regression tests added for all three. Full suite: 892/892 passing.
postGoLivePunchListReconciliation.verifiedNotFixed: Confirmed via direct code read that DATABASE_URL is absent from the live Netlify project's environment variables (production) -- every Postgres-backed function is unreachable in production until it's set; this requires Dylan directly (entering a live database credential is outside what Claude will do regardless of authorization). Local dev Neon database itself verified healthy: 42 tables, all expected tables present, real smoke-test data (12 orgs, 11 tickets, 65 audit_events) -- the database is ready, only the Netlify environment variable is missing. care-hub-app npm audit re-verified: 0 vulnerabilities (was 1 High + 1 Moderate dev-only before the Vite 8 upgrade in ddc2cad).
postGoLivePunchListReconciliation.stillOpen: CH-P0-03 (NETLIFY_BLOBS_TOKEN provider-side rotation -- needs Dylan), CH-H-02's cross-provider portion (invitation-accept.js's Blobs+Postgres span needs a real saga/outbox design, not attempted), CH-H-03 (Square still a static link), CH-H-04 (Cloudinary not integrated), CH-M-01 (frontend/backend contract drift, not re-verified), CH-M-03 (no e2e/a11y suite), CH-M-04 (generic webhook verifier). Attorney review of merged legal content and further Care Hub UI screens beyond dashboard/tickets/checklists were explicitly deferred by Dylan's direction this session (hold at current UI scope until real usage informs what's next).

postV25NativeDevelopment.status: This corpus (docs/development/) was not updated between 2026-07-16 and today -- ongoing work continued directly on main, tracked via this repo's root CLAUDE.md and Claude's own memory system instead of a new dated sessions/SESSION_21_*.md entry. Added while investigating unrelated admin.html-removal work turned up several concretely stale claims elsewhere in this corpus.
postV25NativeDevelopment.whatShippedSinceWithoutASessionLogEntry: The Care Hub UI build-out was completed for all 24 capabilities, not just tickets/checklists (commit 34b56a1, 2026-07-16); admin-images.js/documents.js moved file storage to Cloudinary (commit 31e303b, 2026-07-16); a v25 Apple-style visual redesign and full i18n removal shipped 2026-07-19 (the site is now English-only -- CH-M-01's implicit 16-language assumption and this corpus's occasional i18n references are stale); 3 more Blobs-backed Care Hub capabilities (Site Content, Image Library, Customer Support) were added 2026-07-19 by reusing the pre-Care-Hub content.js/admin-images.js/documents.js/messages.js/notifications.js functions as-is; admin.html was deleted entirely the same day and a new Care Hub /reset-password route was built to replace its one unique capability (staff/admin self-service password recovery, which otherwise would have had no replacement); the session cookie TTL changed from the 8-hour value described elsewhere in this file to 30 days so a signed-in customer stays signed in until they explicitly sign out.
postV25NativeDevelopment.unresolvedAndNeedsDirectReverification: postGoLivePunchListReconciliation (above) found DATABASE_URL absent from Netlify's production environment variables on 2026-07-16 -- every Postgres-backed Care Hub endpoint (organizations, tickets, checklists, approvals, and everything else in this file's endpoint list) would be unreachable for real production users until that's set. Nothing found while writing this note confirms it was ever added since, and it was deliberately not checked via netlify env:list/env:get here (this exact project has a prior incident of that command printing secret values into a session transcript). Dylan should confirm directly in the Netlify dashboard (Site settings -> Environment variables, production context) rather than this note being trusted either way.

currentWave: Session 20 began executing Dylan's 7-decision directive (React/Vite Care Hub UI, TOTP MFA, customer-editable checklists, platform_admin ticket access, legal drafts, Square Sandbox integration, audit-log endpoint) in the specified 10-step order. Steps 1-2 are DONE and live-verified: platform_admin now has every technician ticket capability cross-organization (rbac.js), tickets.js's list-action routing bug is fixed, ticketStore.js/ticketWorkflowStore.js now record real audit events on every write (previously zero audit integration existed for tickets), and a new platform_admin-only audit-log.js endpoint exists with cursor pagination, org/actor/action/date-range filters, and self-auditing access -- 24 endpoints now exist. Steps 3-10 (TOTP MFA, the React/Vite Care Hub itself, Square/email integration, legal drafts, and full accessibility/security/e2e testing) are NOT started -- each is substantial enough to need its own dedicated session(s); see sessions/SESSION_20_RBAC_AND_AUDIT_ENDPOINT.md for the explicit scope decision and what's left.
workspaceNote: This workspace (LTS Stand Alone Software) is a full copy of Development/Business Website/Website Code/v23, including .git and origin remote. v23 itself was not modified. Standing instruction: everything stays local to this workspace for the entire build -- no push, no merge into a new vN folder or main. Session 17 began under a very large autonomous-continuation directive (17 endpoints, retention/deletion, privacy/consent, remote-support boundaries, F060 AI, F001-F060 workbook regeneration); Session 17 delivered invite-only registration, Session 18 the commercial flow, Session 19 (this one) the remaining 12 endpoints -- completing the endpoint-building portion of that directive. Dylan then asked (this session) for a consolidated list of every remaining decision, to be delivered instead of further silent assumptions, before finishing the rest. MERGE (2026-07-15, commit 396008f): Dylan uploaded a fresh copy of the real v23 (its own git repo, pushed to origin/main, 69 commits ahead of this branch's 1570734 fork point -- a completed 8-session audit, Website Designer redesign, new worksheet-handoff flow, security headers) into this workspace as v23/. Added it as a temporary git remote and merged its main into feature/business-care-hub (removed the remote after). 5 files needed manual conflict resolution (package.json, privacy.html, docs/audit/AUDIT_INDEX.md, docs/audit/AUDIT_STATE.json, package-lock.json regenerated); everything else auto-merged cleanly across 361 branch-only + 96 real-site-only changed files. 969/969 tests passing post-merge. See DECISION_LOG.md and the 396008f commit message for full detail. The v23/ subfolder itself is untracked scratch material (Dylan's upload) -- not part of this repo, left in place for reference.

This session (19): 12 more endpoints -- service-records.js (F010), website-profiles.js (F031), entitlements.js (F049), subscriptions.js (F052), technology-assets.js (F043/F041), reminders.js (F048/F037), it-support.js (F044), checklists.js (F046/F047), work-log.js (F025), ticket-workflow.js (F020/F021/F022), activity-timeline.js (F017), metrics.js/templates.js/webhook-events.js (F054/F055/F057). Also: 8 new rbac.js view capabilities for customer roles, history.view extended to all three customer roles, reminderStore.listRemindersForOrganization added (a real gap), and a real production bug found and fixed via live testing (triage_results.matched_rule_id type mismatch, migration 003).

releaseRecommendation: NOT READY -- and materially less ready than this field claimed earlier today. CORRECTION (see SECURITY_REVIEW.md's 'Correction' section for full detail): step 10's 'RBAC/org-scoping/IDOR: clean' conclusion was WRONG. An independently-authored review found, and this session independently verified and fixed, a real Critical cross-tenant IDOR: approvals.js's PATCH handler authorized the caller against a client-supplied organizationId but fetched/updated the actual approval_requests row by id alone -- an org_owner of Org A supplying Org B's approvalId could read AND MUTATE Org B's approval. Three more read endpoints (scope-of-work.js, change-orders.js, payment-requests.js) had the identical bug (query by child id/subject alone, no organization_id predicate). All four fixed (organization_id now required and enforced in every affected query); 3 new regression tests added; 791/791 passing. Do not trust this document's or SECURITY_REVIEW.md's earlier 'clean' language for anything predating this correction. Every non-Square item of Dylan's original 10-step directive is otherwise done. 25 endpoints exist. TOTP MFA is built and live-verified, with MFA_ENCRYPTION_KEY now set on the live Netlify site (production context, write-only secret). Tickets/checklists work end to end for both customers and staff. Step 10's full application security review ALSO fixed a systemic missing-audit-trail gap across 12 store files, and fixed a TOTP replay vulnerability -- those findings and fixes stand uncorrected. Step 10's one Critical finding -- mfa-enroll.js had no defense against a password-only compromise hijacking the FIRST TOTP enrollment -- now has a REAL PREVENTIVE FIX (built after Dylan said 'go for it'): confirm defers activation to a single-use 30-minute emailed confirmation link by default (new action: verify-email), falling back to the pre-fix immediate-activation behavior only when email genuinely can't be delivered. RESEND_API_KEY/EMAIL_FROM were discovered to already be live on this site (pre-existing from an earlier feature), so this fix is materially closed for this deployment, not just for a future one. privacy.html and terms.html were merged with the Care Hub's real data-collection/sub-processor disclosures per Dylan's explicit direction ('merge everything') -- this closes audit findings F006 and F007 (marked Resolved in docs/audit/AUDIT_STATE.json). terms.html gained a Care Hub Accounts section (Sec 18) that incorporates the site's existing liability/indemnification/governing-law sections rather than inventing new ones, per Dylan's direction to use 'full scale protections... as a placeholder.' The docs/development/legal/ drafts are now superseded working notes pointing to the merged pages. Cloudinary was decided as the object-storage provider (owner decision #11) -- not yet integrated. A static Square Payment Link was wired as a manual, non-integrated development stopgap (a 'Make a payment' card on the customer Dashboard) -- NOT a real Square API integration; that remains not started, blocked on Dylan's checkout-flow input. One operational incident this session: netlify env:get/env:list --json print values in plaintext regardless of --secret; several throwaway MFA_ENCRYPTION_KEY candidates and the site's real LTS_SESSION_SECRET/NETLIFY_BLOBS_TOKEN were accidentally printed to the session transcript before this was understood (the final live MFA_ENCRYPTION_KEY value itself was never exposed). Dylan was told to rotate the two exposed pre-existing secrets; this session did not do so itself since rotating LTS_SESSION_SECRET signs out current live-site sessions, a real user-facing effect outside unprompted authorization. Full suite: 791/791 passing (up from 763 at the start of steps 9/10/8, including the post-correction IDOR-fix regression tests), npm audit: 0 vulnerabilities in both workspaces. NEW gap from the correction: the same review flagged that nearly every store in this codebase does select-decide-unconditional-UPDATE without a SQL transaction (a concurrent duplicate decision could theoretically race) -- a pre-existing, codebase-wide pattern, not fixed this pass, needs a dedicated session. Real, tracked gaps remaining: attorney review of the now-live merged legal content (merged is not the same as reviewed); professional translation of the merged privacy.html/terms.html content into the 15 non-English languages the site already supports (this session updated the English source only -- a real accuracy gap for non-English visitors, not cosmetic); QR-code rendering for MFA enrollment; an organization-directory picker for staff; a single-ticket-detail route; a live-backend browser smoke test of the full sign-in path; Medium/Low security findings from step 10 (free-text length caps, dormant CSV formula-injection gap, generic error-catch pattern, rate-limit race, MFA recovery-exhaustion path); the retention/deletion/legal-hold system as enforced code; remote-support/automation boundary enforcement; a real dynamic Square integration; F060 AI; F001-F060 workbook regeneration; and accessibility/e2e testing.
